name: Netlogon denied a vulnerable secure channel connection (trust)
definition: Windows Security Event ID 5828 records Netlogon denied a vulnerable secure channel connection (trust).
trigger_scenarios: Windows logs the event when the audited security operation occurs.

key_fields.explanation: A denied vulnerable trust secure channel shows blocked Zerologon-style trust abuse; repeated attempts from a trust partner within window=15 minutes require patch review.
The initiating account and Logon ID support attribution and correlation to 4624 and 4688.
The affected host determines the operational impact.

false_positives: Documented maintenance, policy changes, or approved administration can generate this event.
Validate change control and host role before suppressing.
detection_notes: A denied vulnerable trust secure channel shows blocked Zerologon-style trust abuse; repeated attempts from a trust partner within window=15 minutes require patch review. This is a T1210 Exploitation of Remote Services signal; preserve the concrete value and correlate the actor, process, and related events.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 5828
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account
